items.title: 'Surreal feeling' | Players, fans and Timothee Chalamet celebrate Knicks' historic win
items.summary: New York Knicks' head coach Mike Brown said it was a 'surreal feeling' after his side beat the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 to clinch the NBA Championship.

items.title: Man Utd in pole position to sign relegated £80m-rated West Ham star Mateus Fernandes ahead of Champions League rivals
items.summary: REAL MADRID expect Manchester United to sign West Ham midfielder Mateus Fernandes. Arsenal have cooled their interest in Fernandes, who has also been linked with Madrid following the return of Jose Mourinho as coach. items.title: Phil Foden gets over his World Cup heartache as he laughs and jokes with stunning Wag ringside for Tommy Fury fight
items.summary: SNUBBED England star Phil Foden got over his World Cup heartache with a trip to the boxing. The Manchester City ace was spotted giggling ringside at Tommy Fury's box-office clash with Eddie Hall on Saturday night. items.title: Jones retains IBF crown after split draw with Carranza
items.summary: Oshae Jones retained the IBF junior middleweight championship against Elia Carranza after judges declared the fight a split draw at MVP Boxing 04 in Orlando.

items.title: Leicester close in on Martin appointment
items.summary: Russell Martin, who led Southampton into the Premier League in 2024, has been out of work since being sacked by Rangers in October.

items.title: Former Premier League boss Roberto Mancini lined up for shock new job after resigning from Qatar league champions
items.summary: ROBERTO MANCINI has QUIT as Al Sadd boss after just eight months in charge, paving the way for a stunning return to one of football's biggest jobs. items.title: World Cup of Darts LIVE! England's Littler, Humphries in quarters after Scotland reach semis
How Cape Verde plan to 'surprise people' at the World Cup
items.summary: Cape Verde’s presence at this World Cup, where they take on favourites Spain in their opening game, is one of the fairy-tales of this tournament. But it has been coming. “I believe we can surprise people once again,” Telmo Arcanjo tells Sky Sports .

items.title: BBC Sport pundits choose England World Cup XIs - who would you pick?
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel will pick his first England World Cup team on Wednesday, when the Three Lions take on Croatia. BBC Sport pundits choose their starting XIs - who would you pick?

items.title: Could Japan be the World Cup's dark horses?
items.summary: Japan could be one of the surprise packages of the 2026 World Cup, with growing belief they are ready to go further than ever before.
